original_abstract: In this study, a cerium-based metal–organic framework (Ce-MOF) was synthesized via a hydrothermal route using 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid as the organic linker. The resulting material, characterized by a high degree of crystallinity and a combination of Ce³⁺ and triphenylamine Lewis acid sites, demonstrates significant Fenton-like catalytic activity. The Ce-MOF was utilized for the catalytic oxidation of various substrates, including olefins, primary and secondary alcohols, and organic dyes. Specifically, the catalyst achieved 100% conversion of cinnamyl alcohol and 53% conversion of styrene, with high selectivity toward benzaldehyde (100%) and styrene oxide (75%), respectively. Additionally, the framework proved highly effective for the advanced oxidation of aqueous pollutants, facilitating the degradation of dyes such as Rhodamine B, Methyl Blue, Congo Red, and Direct Blue in the presence of hydrogen peroxide. Mechanistic investigations confirmed that the catalytic process is driven by the generation of reactive hydroxyl radicals (•OH). Furthermore, the Ce-MOF exhibited excellent durability, maintaining its catalytic performance over five consecutive cycles. These findings highlight the potential of cerium-based MOFs as robust and recyclable heterogeneous catalysts for fine chemical synthesis and environmental remediation.
humanized_abstract: Herein, we present a new cerium-based metal-organic framework (Ce-MOF) material synthesized through hydrothermal reaction using 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid as the organic ligand, exhibiting pronounced Fenton-like catalytic activities. The highly crystallized Ce-MOF catalyst containing Ce3+ sites and active triphenylamine Lewis acid sites showed excellent catalytic activities towards the oxidation of various olefins, primary/secondary alcohols and organic dyes. Complete conversion of cinnamyl alcohol and 53% conversion of styrene with 100% and 75% selectivities toward benzaldehyde and styrene oxide, respectively, were achieved. Meanwhile, the heterogeneous catalyst effectively induced the advanced oxidation of various aqueous dyes such as Rhodamine B, Methyl Blue, Congo Red and Direct Blue under hydrogen peroxide assistance. The generation of hydroxyl radicals, serving as the active oxidants for the catalytic oxidation, was verified by relevant experiments. The recycling performance of the catalyst toward heterogeneous catalytic oxidation was also evaluated. The as-prepared catalyst retained good catalytic performance after five rounds of recycling. The obtained results not only validate the catalytic activities toward fine chemical synthesis of heterogeneous cerium-based MOFs but also endorse their promising applications in water remediation and pollution control.
